explanation: The primary diagnosis is cervical radiculopathy, with myelopathy documented only as a secondary/incidental finding. Per the NOTE under 2.2, the myelopathy exception applies only when myelopathy is the primary diagnosis, so standard conservative treatment requirements apply. The member fails the standard requirements: PT was only 4 weeks (below the 6-week minimum), and no interventional treatment (2.2(c)) was performed, with the waiver inapplicable because the myelopathy exception is not invoked. Therefore, Section 2 criteria are not satisfied and authorization is denied.
